LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FAIR TITLING TRUST'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. THE ABRAMSON DECLARATION SHOWS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET AND CONFERRED IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41. PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH THE CODE AND THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE. DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE TO THE COMPLAINT IS NOW DUE BY SEPTEMBER 1, 2023. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FAIR TITLING TRUST DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Defendant Fair Titling Trust's demurrer, which is unopposed, is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-02-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Michael A. Abramson, Esq. (310) 247-9302 maa@abramsonlawgroup.com for Plaintiff FAIR TITLING TRUST,
No other appearances

Plaitnff's counsel does not contest the Court's tentative ruing.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FAIR TITLING TRUST's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

Off calendar. The Abramson declaration shows that plaintiff has not met and conferred in compliance with CCP 430.41. Plaintiff is ordered to comply with the code and the parties are ordered to meet and confer in person or by telephone. Defendant's response to the complaint is now due by September 1, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
